'''Danny Brough''' (born 15 January 1983) is a Scottish former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who is the head coach of the [[Huddersfield R.U.F.C.|Huddersfield Rugby Union Club]]. He played as a {{rlp|so|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}} mainly known for his spell with the town's Rugby League club, [[Huddersfield Giants]] where he was a mainstay for a decade and captain of the Giants throughout their most successful modern era spell, during which he became the first player to win the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]] twice and only the second Giants player to be named [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] after [[Brett Hodgson]] in 2009.

Brough started his professional career in 2002 with [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the second tier before moving to [[York City Knights]] a year later. Brough made his [[Super League]] debut in 2005 after signing for [[Hull F.C.]], winning the [[Challenge Cup]] final with the club in the same season. After spending a season with [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2007's [[RFL Championship|National League One]], he returned to the Super League with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] before switching to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2010. After a second spell with Wakefield, he finished his career at [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2021.

Brough also played at international level for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], being eligible to play for the latter through his grandfather, and won a Scottish record 24 caps.

==Background==
Brough was born in [[Thornhill, West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Domestic career===
====Dewsbury Rams====
Brough started his playing career as a junior at Thornhill before signing for [[Wakefield Trinity]] at the age of 15.<ref name="delariviere">{{cite web|last1=de la Rivière|first1=Richard|title=Danny Brough (1)|url=http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=105|publisher=WordPress|access-date=19 May 2015|date=27 December 2010|archive-date=6 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150706182643/http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=105|url-status=dead}}</ref> He failed to make a first team appearance for the club and moved to [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] in the [[Northern Ford Premiership]], making his senior début in 2002.<ref name="delariviere2">{{cite web|last1=de la Rivière|first1=Richard|title=Danny Brough (2)|url=http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=107|publisher=WordPress|access-date=19 May 2015|date=27 December 2010|archive-date=6 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150706165640/http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=107|url-status=dead}}</ref> Seeking more regular first team opportunities, Brough requested a transfer during the 2003 season, and was subsequently sold to [[RFL League 1|National League Two]] side [[York City Knights]] after two seasons at the Dewsbury Rams.<ref>{{cite web|title=Brough leaves – Thaler loan deal|url=http://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/local-sport/brough-leaves-thaler-loan-deal-1-1349699|website=Dewsbury Reporter|access-date=19 May 2015|date=1 May 2003}}</ref>

====York City Knights====
In 2004, Brough set new club records at York for most goals scored in a season (178), and most points scored in a season (412),<ref>{{cite web|title=Brough's flight from Knights|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/7877018.broughs-flight-from-knights/|website=York Press|access-date=24 December 2023|date=18 October 2004 }}</ref>{{efn|Both these records stood until 2018 when broken by [[Connor Robinson]] (186 goals and 420 points)<ref name=crowned2018>{{cite web|url= https://yorkcityknights.com/reports/knights-crowned-champions-whitehaven-win/ |title=Knights crowned champions after Whitehaven win: Sunday 23rd September - League 1 |website=yorkcityknights.com |date=24 September 2018 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210117082404/https://yorkcityknights.com/reports/knights-crowned-champions-whitehaven-win/ |archive-date=17 January 2021}}</ref>}} helping the club reach the quarter-final of the [[Challenge Cup]],<ref name="delariviere2" /> and narrowly missing out on promotion to [[RFL Championship|National League One]] following a 30–34 defeat in the play-off final against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. After just two seasons he was signed by [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] after making 53 appearances for York.

====Hull FC====
Brough had a dream start at Hull, scoring four goals and a drop-goal in Hull's [[2005 Challenge Cup#Final|2005 Challenge Cup Final]] win over [[Leeds Rhinos]], with many claiming he was unlucky not to win the [[Lance Todd Trophy]]. After an impressive first season, he signed a new two-year contract with the club.<ref>{{cite web |title=Brough extends contract at Hull |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/4449094.stm |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=20 January 2022 |date=18 November 2005}}</ref> During the following season, Brough lost his place in the starting lineup after the arrival of new head coach [[Peter Sharp (rugby league)|Peter Sharp]], and was signed by [[Castleford Tigers]] for an undisclosed fee in June 2006.<ref>{{cite web |title=Brough seals move to Castleford |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/5086936.stm |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=20 January 2022 |date=16 June 2006}}</ref>

====Castleford Tigers====
Brough played 10 games for Castleford in 2006, but was unable to prevent the team from being relegated from the Super League. He remained with Castleford in 2007 and was part of the team that won promotion from [[RFL Championship|National League One]] back into the Super League.
[[File:Danny Brough.jpg|thumb|right|Brough kicking at goal for Wakefield]]

====Wakefield Trinity====
Following Castleford's promotion, Brough was signed by rivals Wakefield Trinity in 2008 and became a key player for the team, going on to win the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]] that year. Brough, again only stayed at Wakefield for two seasons before leaving at the end of 2009 to join West Yorkshire rivals Huddersfield. He made 57 appearances for Trinity scoring 468 points.

====Huddersfield Giants====
In March 2010, Brough was signed by [[Huddersfield Giants]] for a fee of £30,000, and agreed to loan [[Danny Kirmond]] to Wakefield until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Huddersfield hungry for honours after signing Danny Brough |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/mar/24/danny-brough-huddersfield-rugby-league |website=The Guardian |access-date=19 January 2022 |date=24 March 2010}}</ref> 
[[File:Danny Brough 2010.jpg|thumb|right|100px|Brough playing for Huddersfield]]
He was named club captain during 2012 following the mid-season departure of [[Kevin Brown (English rugby league)|Kevin Brown]].<ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_10144-brough-named-as-huddersfield-captain.html| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20130922071928/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_10144-brough-named-as-huddersfield-captain.html| archive-date = 2013-09-22| title = Brough named as Huddersfield captain}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/8568233.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Brough completes move to Giants|date=24 March 2010}}</ref>

At the end of the 2013 Super League season, Brough won the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] award for his excellent performances throughout the season. He also became the first player ever to win a second [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]].<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014|archive-date=26 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
[[File:Danny Brough and Ian Henderson 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|right|Brough and [[Ian Henderson (rugby league)|Ian Henderson]] playing for Scotland in [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]]]

====Wakefield Trinity====
Brough returned to Wakefield in 2019.

He left at the end of the 2020 season.

====Bradford Bulls====
In June 2020, it was announced that Brough would link up with former coach [[John Kear]] on a 2-year deal in the [[RFL Championship]] with the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18534785.bulls-sign-experienced-half-back-brough-two-year-deal/|title=Bulls to sign experienced half back Brough on two-year deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 June 2020 |access-date=24 November 2020}}</ref> Brough announced his retirement in November 2021.<ref>{{cite web |title=Danny Brough retires |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36221/danny-brough-retires |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=19 January 2022 |date=1 November 2021}}</ref>

===International career===
====Scotland====
Brough played at international level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], qualifying via his grandfather, making his international début in 2004 and playing in sporadic competitions and test matches.

In 2008 he was called up to the Scotland squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], and was named captain for the first time. Brough's international future was thrown into question following the World Cup, as he announced his intention to switch allegiance to [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2009, citing a lack of opportunities to play in major tournaments with Scotland.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://metro.co.uk/2009/02/06/brough-targets-england-spot-434709/|title = Brough targets England spot|date = 6 February 2009}}</ref> However, Brough was not selected for England, and did not feature at international level for two years.

In 2013, Brough reverted to Scotland and captained the team during the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]]. After the tournament, Brough was shortlisted alongside [[Sonny Bill Williams]] and [[Greg Inglis]] for the [[RLIF Awards#International Player of the Year|RLIF International Player of the Year]].<ref>[http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls/burgess-brothers-join-brough-to-receive-top-accolades-on-proud-night-for-dewsbury-s-league-trio-1-6281473 Burgess brothers join Brough to receive top accolades on proud night for Dewsbury’s league trio], by Dave Craven, yorkshirepost.co.uk, dated 28 November 2013.</ref>

In October 2014, Brough led Scotland in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]].<ref>{{cite news
|title = Huddersfield captain Danny Brough believes Scotland need to play at a higher level
|publisher = The Huddersfield Daily Examiner
|date = 2014-10-17
|url = http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-captain-danny-brough-7947230
|access-date = 2015-09-30}}</ref> The Winner of the tournament would qualify to play in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]] alongside International heavyweights Australia, England and New Zealand. He played at his best throughout the tournament, earning him the 'Player of the Tournament' award.<ref>{{cite news
|title = Brough crowned Player of the Tournament
|publisher = rlef.eu.com
|date = 2014-11-07
|url = http://www.rlef.eu.com/news/article/947/brough-crowned-player-of-the
|access-date = 2015-09-30}}</ref> His performances and leadership inspired the team to qualify for 2016's major international rugby league competition and win the [[Rugby League European Cup|European Cup]] for the first ever time. Later in the year, his performances made him the clear winner for the 'Prince of Scots' award as the best player to play in the Bravehearts' jersey that year.<ref>{{cite news
 |title        = Brough crowned Prince of Scots
 |publisher    = scotlandrl
 |date         = 2014-12-31
 |url          = http://www.scotlandrl.com/article/12718/brough-crowned-prince-of-scots
 |access-date   = 2015-10-01
 |archive-url  = https://web.archive.org/web/20151002061431/http://www.scotlandrl.com/article/12718/brough-crowned-prince-of-scots
 |archive-date = 2 October 2015
 |url-status   = dead
}}</ref>

Again in October and November 2015, Brough decided to play for Scotland instead of taking a long off-season break and was therefore named as the captain of the Bravehearts in their [[2015 European Cup]] campaign.<ref>{{cite news
|title = Danny Brough to lead Scotland in European Championships
|publisher = EUROSPORT
|date = 2015-09-30
|url = http://asia.eurosport.com/rugby-league/danny-brough-to-lead-scotland-in-european-championships_sto4931832/story.shtml
|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20151001045509/http://asia.eurosport.com/rugby-league/danny-brough-to-lead-scotland-in-european-championships_sto4931832/story.shtml
|url-status = dead
|archive-date = 1 October 2015
|access-date = 2015-09-30}}</ref>

Five months before the 2016 Four Nations, Scotland announced Brough would captain the Bravehearts in their first tournament (other than the World Cup) against the 'big 3' international teams.<ref>{{cite news
 |title        = DANNY TO LEAD SCOTLAND AGAINST WORLD CHAMPIONS
 |publisher    = SCOTLAND RUGBY LEAGUE
 |date         = 2016-05-19
 |url          = http://scotlandrl.com/article/14492/danny-to-lead-scotland-against
 |access-date   = 2016-05-19
 |archive-url  = https://web.archive.org/web/20160603072951/http://scotlandrl.com/article/14492/danny-to-lead-scotland-against
 |archive-date = 3 June 2016
 |url-status   = dead
}}</ref>

In 2017 Brough captained Scotland's squad in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]]. He played in the matches against Tonga and New Zealand, but was then released from the squad and sent home before their final group match, after being deemed too drunk to board a flight in Christchurch.

====England====
Brough was eventually called into the England squad for an [[International Origin]] match in 2012. He was subsequently omitted once again from the England squad, and, amid heavy speculation, in 2013 announced that he would once again be committing to Scotland, having not featured for England once.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-reaction/37182/brough-will-play-for-scotland |title=Sporting Life – Rugby League News | Live Scores, Super League, Challenge Cup |access-date=4 June 2013 |archive-date=15 June 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130615131430/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-reaction/37182/brough-will-play-for-scotland |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Records and statistics==
With a combined total of 3,987 points at club and representative level, Brough is the fifth highest points scorer in British rugby league history (behind [[Neil Fox (rugby league)|Neil Fox]], [[Jim Sullivan (Welsh rugby league)|Jim Sullivan]], [[Kevin Sinfield]] and [[Gus Risman]]).<ref>{{cite web |title=Danny Brough reveals rugby union offer that came and went as he heads off into retirement |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-brough-reveals-rugby-union-offer-that-came-and-went-as-he-heads-off-into-retirement-3440919 |website=The Yorkshire Post |access-date=5 July 2022 |date=1 November 2021}}</ref>
